---
title: "Large Cohort Transcriptomics"
subtitle: "Adelaide Biomed Seminar Series"
author: "Dr Stevie Pederson (They/Them)<br>Black Ochre Data Labs<br>The Kids Research Institute Australia / ANU"
date: "2026-03-13"
date-format: long
title-slide-attributes:
    data-background-color: "#3D3D40"
    data-background-opacity: "70%"
    data-background-size: "20%"
    data-background-position: "95% 10%"
editor: source
format: 
  revealjs:
    bibliography: references.bib
    theme: [assets/bodl.scss]
    code-line-numbers: false
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    sansfont: Times New Roman
    logo: assets/bodl_logo_white_background.jpg
    slide-number: c/t
include-after: |
  <script type="text/javascript">
    Reveal.on('ready', event => {
      if (event.indexh === 0) {
        document.querySelector("div.has-logo > img.slide-logo").style.display = "none";
      }
    });
    Reveal.addEventListener('slidechanged', (event) => {
      if (event.indexh === 0) {
        Reveal.configure({ slideNumber: null });
        document.querySelector("div.has-logo > img.slide-logo").style.display = "none";
      }
      if (event.indexh === 1) { 
        Reveal.configure({ slideNumber: 'c' });
        document.querySelector("div.has-logo > img.slide-logo").style.display = null;
      }
    });
  </script>    
knitr: 
  opts_chunk: 
    echo: false
    include: true
    warning: false
    message: false
    fig.align: left
    fig.width: 8
    fig.heght: 6
---

```{r packages, echo = FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(bodlR)
library(reactable)
library(htmltools)
theme_set(theme_bodl())
```


##

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="35%"}

### Acknowledgement of Country

![](images/karrawirraparri.png)

:::

::: {.column width="65%"}

I would like to acknowledge that many of us are meeting today on Kaurna Country (Karrawirraparri: Redgum Forest River).<br>

I acknowledge the deep feelings of attachment and relationship of the Kaurna people to their Place.<br><br>

I also pay my respects to the cultural authority of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples from other areas of Australia online today, and pay my respects to Elders past, present and emerging. 


:::

:::

::: {.notes}
I also wish to express how grateful I am for the opportunity to be involved in this space.
I'm acutely aware of the trust that has been placed in me and am very pleased to be surrounded by so many wise & experienced researchers
:::



## About Me

- Did 1st year science in 1986 $\rightarrow$ completed 2002-2005 (Genetics)
    + BMa.Comp.Sc. (Hons) 2006-2007
    + 1988-1991: Elder Conservatorium
    
::: {.fragment}
- First came across R (1.5.1) analysing two-colour microarrays in Dec 2002

![https://deepwiki.com/cran/limma/1.1-package-history-and-evolution](images/limma.png){fig-align="left"}
:::

## About Me

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width=55%}

- PhD 2008-2018
    + Normal student: 2008-2011
    + Homeless/Couch-surfing: 2012-2013
    + Bioinformatics Hub: 2014-2020
    
::: {.incremental}
- 2020-2022: Dame Roma Mitchell Cancer Research Labs
- 2022-2026: Black Ochre Data Labs
:::
    
::: 

::: {.column width=45%}    
![](images/IMG_5317.JPG){height=300px}
![](images/IMG_5349.JPG){height=300px}
:::    

::::    
    
## Bioconductor Enthusiast

![](https://smped.github.io/ngsReports/reference/figures/ngsReports.png){.absolute left=0 top=100 width='150px'}

![](https://smped.github.io/extraChIPs/reference/figures/extraChIPs.png){.absolute left=200 top=100 width='150px'}

![](https://smped.github.io/motifTestR/reference/figures/favicon.png){.absolute left=400 top=100 width='150px'}

![](https://smped.github.io/transmogR/reference/figures/transmogR.png){.absolute left=600 top=100 width='150px'}

![](https://smped.github.io/SimpleUpset/reference/figures/SimpleUpset.png){.absolute right=100 top=100 width='150px'}
<br><br><br><br>

- Also developed & maintain `strandcheckR` (Hien To)
    + Helped with `sSNAPPY` (Nora Liu) + `tadar` (Lachlan Baer)
- Currently co-chair of Community Advisory Board 
    + Leading BiocAsia Working Group

![](https://bioconductor.org/images/cab/LoriShepherd.png){.absolute right=0 bottom=100 width='150px'}
![](https://bioconductor.org/images/cab/KevinRueAlbrecht.png){.absolute right=200 bottom=100 width='150px'}

# The PROPHECY Study {background-color="#3d3d40" background-image=images/prophecy.png background-size="100%" background-opacity="0.3" background-position='40% 50%'}


## The PROPHECY Study

![](images/life_expectancy.png)


## The PROPHECY Study

![](images/t2d_burden.png)


## The PROPHECY Study

::: {.notes}
- A key design element is that all research is community led, not researcher led
- 
:::

![](images/prophecy_layers.png)

::: {.fragment .fade-in-then-out data-fragment-index="1"}

::: {.rect_outline .absolute top='220px' left='240px' width='120px' height='80px'}
:::

:::


## The PROPHECY Study

- A key design element $\rightarrow$ all research is community led, not researcher led
- Prof Alex Brown spent 5 years consulting with SA communities first
- Strong Indigenous Data Sovereignty Principles
- Strict data management protocols
    + High demand for clear documentation

![](https://2020.diabetescongress.com.au/uploads/images/speakers/_speaker/Alex-Brown.jpg){.absolute right=20 bottom=100 width='350px'}

## Clickbait Time

Is large cohort transcriptomics just scRNA without the zero counts?

. . .

![Stevie speaks the truth](https://media.giphy.com/media/v1.Y2lkPWVjZjA1ZTQ3N3Q5cW0wMXloa2J2dzFwNTZqMm00NmE2cXV3cm5uZWVnOXlxM3hneCZlcD12MV9naWZzX3JlbGF0ZWQmY3Q9Zw/26hkhKd2Cp5WMWU1O/giphy.gif){fig-align="left"}

# The PROPHECY Transcriptomics Layer {background-color="#3d3d40" background-image=images/prophecy.png background-size="100%" background-opacity="0.3" background-position='40% 50%'}

## Sequencing Considerations

::: {.notes}
- Previously, I'd only done n = 4, with up to 4 treatment groups
:::

- Whole Blood from participants $\implies$ incredibly valuable resource
- Trying to to get everything as 'right' as possible $\implies$ no second chances
- 13x96 well plates were planned for & paid before I joined $\implies$ N = 1248

::: {.fragment .fade-in-then-out data-fragment-index="1"}

![](https://static1.cbrimages.com/wordpress/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/far-side-roger-screws-up.jpg){.absolute width='350px'}

:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in data-fragment-index="2"}

- Subset of 93 participants already sequenced by the El Osta group
    + Focus on Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) + Diabetes (T2D)
- Total RNA $\implies$ rRNA Depleted **but not** hbRNA depleted
    + hbRNA was 30-50% of library
    + rRNA consistently below 20% (mostly below 10%)
- Pilot study compared polyA with totalRNA (rRNA + hbRNA depleted)

:::

## Study Design

- Prepared in batches of 96 $\implies$ batch effects 
    + All reagents from same supplier batches
- Chose a design strategy compatible with hRUV [@Kim2021-kz]
    + Short replicates, Long replicates, Pools
- 1134 Blood samples were selected for inclusion based on:
    + RIN >5.0 & RNA concentration > 25ng/&#956;l 
- Samples are stored in extraction order with a ULN
    + Randomising samples would be extremely difficult
    + Only had access to RIN, Concentration & Sex
    + El Osta sub-cohort were all late ULNs

## Experimental Design

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column}

```{r plate-df}
set.seed(11)
plate_df <- tibble(
  plate = "Plate i",
  col = rep_len(LETTERS[1:8], 96) %>% as.factor(),
  row = rep(1:12, each = 8) %>% as.factor(),
  pool = FALSE,
  short = FALSE,
  long = seq_along(plate) %in% c(1:3, sample(4:96, 3)),
  library = ifelse(long, paste("Long Rep", seq_along(plate)), NA_character_)
)
set.seed(101)
short <- plate_df %>% 
  dplyr::filter(!long) %>% 
  sample_n(6) %>% 
  arrange(col, row) %>% 
  mutate(
    short = TRUE,
    library = rep_len(paste("Short Rep", 1:3), 6)
  )
set.seed(1010)
pools <- plate_df %>% 
  dplyr::filter(!long) %>% 
  anti_join(short) %>% 
  sample_n(3) %>% 
  arrange(col, row) %>% 
  mutate(
    pool = TRUE,
    library = paste0("Pool", 1:3)
  )
final_plate <- plate_df %>% 
  anti_join(short, by = join_by(plate, col, row, pool, short, long, library)) %>% 
  anti_join(pools, by = join_by(plate, col, row, pool, short, long, library)) %>% 
  bind_rows(short) %>% 
  bind_rows(pools) %>% 
  arrange(col, row) %>% 
  mutate(
    library = ifelse(is.na(library), paste("Participant", seq_along(library)), library),
    type = case_when(
      pool ~ "Pool",
      short ~ "Short Replicate",
      long ~ "Long Replicate",
      TRUE ~ "Participant Sample"
    ) %>% fct_infreq(),
    x = as.integer(col), 
    y = row %>% fct_rev() %>% as.integer()
  )
```


```{r plate-long}
final_plate %>% 
  ggplot(aes(col, fct_rev(row))) +
  geom_point(
    shape = 21, size = 8, fill = "grey70"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(fill = type),
    data = . %>% dplyr::filter(long) ,
    shape = 21, size = 8
  ) +
  geom_curve(
    aes(x = -1, xend = x, y = rnd, yend = y),
    data = . %>% 
      dplyr::filter(long, y == 12) %>% 
      mutate(rnd = sample(1:12, 3)),
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.03, "npc")),
    curvature = -1/4
  ) +
  geom_curve(
    aes(x = x, xend = 8.6, y = y, yend = 12),
    data = . %>% 
      dplyr::filter(long, y != 12),
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.03, "npc")),
    curvature = -1/10
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~plate) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1, 8)) +
  scale_fill_bodl(name = "Library Type", values = -1)
```

:::

::: {.column}
- Plates include:
    + 6 *Long Replicates* to straddle consecutive plates
    
:::
::::

::: {.fragment}

### Trivia Time!!!

:::

::: {.fragment}
- Did you know that plates are prepared using a multi-tip pipette?
- Ordered by columns not rows

:::

## Experimental Design

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column}

```{r plate-short}
final_plate %>% 
  ggplot(aes(col, fct_rev(row))) +
  geom_point(
    shape = 21, size = 8, fill = "grey70"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(fill = type),
    data = . %>% dplyr::filter(short | long),
    shape = 21, size = 8
  ) +
  geom_curve(
    aes(
      x = x_FALSE, xend = x_TRUE, y = y_FALSE, yend = y_TRUE
    ),
    data = . %>% 
      dplyr::filter(short) %>% 
      mutate(dup = duplicated(library)) %>% 
      pivot_wider(names_from = dup, values_from = c(x, y), id_cols = library),
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.03, "npc")),
    curvature = -1/2
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~plate) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1, 8)) +
  scale_fill_bodl(name = "Library Type", values = -1)
```

:::

::: {.column}
- Plates include:
    + 6 *Long Replicates* to straddle consecutive plates
    + 6 *Short Replicates* within plates
:::

::::

## Experimental Design

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column}

```{r plate-pools}
final_plate %>% 
  ggplot(aes(col, fct_rev(row))) +
  geom_point(
    shape = 21, size = 8, fill = "grey70"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(fill = type),
    data = . %>% dplyr::filter(short | long | pool),
    shape = 21, size = 8
  ) +
  geom_curve(
    aes(x = -1, xend = x, y = rnd, yend = y),
    data = . %>% 
      dplyr::filter(pool) %>% 
      mutate(rnd = sample(1:12, 3)),
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.03, "npc")),
    curvature = -1/12
  ) +
  geom_curve(
    aes(x = x, xend = 8.6, y = y, yend = rnd),
    data = . %>% 
      dplyr::filter(pool) %>% 
      mutate(rnd = sample(1:12, 3)),
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.03, "npc")),
    curvature = -1/10
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~plate) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1, 8)) +
  scale_fill_bodl(name = "Library Type", values = -1)
```

:::

::: {.column}
- Plates include:
    + 6 *Long Replicates* to straddle consecutive plates
    + 6 *Short Replicates* within plates
    + 3 distinct *pools* across all 13 plates
    + 87 Participants $\implies$ 1,134 total
    
:::

::::

## Data Processing Workflow

- Data processing written in `nextflow`
    + Dr Alastair Ludington & Holly Massacci
- Run as every plate came in
- Produced a set of plate-level QC reports
    + Threw everything at it: `fastp`, `RSeQC`, `samtools stats`, all log files etc
    + Forgot to include `strandcheckR` [@To2019-rj] $\rightarrow$ supplementary pipeline
- Manually checked every plate for Pass/Fail/Caution
    + Thinking about which libraries to resequence
    
![](images/holly.png){.absolute right=280 top=80 width='250px'}
![](images/archer.png){.absolute right=0 top=80 width='250px'}

## Data Processing Workflow

```{r pipeline, out.width='100%'}
library(DiagrammeR)

grViz("
digraph {

  # a 'graph' statement
  graph [overlap = true, fontsize = 10, newrank = true, ordering='in']

  subgraph cluster_QC {

    # Key Data Nodes
    node [shape = box, penwidth = 3]
    FQ_PROC [label = 'Trimmed & \nDeDuplicated FQ']

    # Minor Data Nodes
    node [shape = box, fontcolor = grey30, color = grey30, penwidth = 1]
    FPJ [label = 'fastp.json'];

    # Process Nodes
    node [shape = ellipse, penwidth = 1, color = red, fontcolor = red]
    FP [label = 'fastp']

    # External Inputs
    node [shape = box, color = navyblue, fontcolor = navyblue, penwidth = 1]
    ADAPTERS [label = 'Adapter\nSequences']

    # Reporting Nodes
    node [shape = diamond, color = forestgreen, fontcolor = forestgreen, penwidth = 2]
    RAW_QC [label = 'Library QC Report']

    ADAPTERS -> FP -> FPJ

    edge [penwidth=1]
    FPJ -> RAW_QC;

    fontsize=30;
    label = 'Library QC';
    color = grey;

  }

  subgraph cluster_depletion {

    # Process Nodes
    node [shape = ellipse, penwidth = 1, color = red, fontcolor = red]
    BWA1 [label = 'bwa']
    BWA2 [label = 'bwa']
    HEAD [label = 'head (bash)']
    FLAGSTAT1 [label = 'samtools flagstat']
    FLAGSTAT2 [label = 'samtools flagstat']

    # Minor Data Nodes
    node [shape = box, fontcolor = grey30, color = grey30, penwidth = 1]
    SUBFQ [label = 'subsample_R1.fastq.gz'];
    RRNABAM [label = 'rrna.bam'];
    HBRNABAM [label = 'hbrna.bam'];
    RRNAFLAGSTATS [label = 'rrna.flagstats'];
    HBRNAFLAGSTATS [label = 'hbrna.flagstats'];

    # External Inputs
    node [shape = box, color = navyblue, fontcolor = navyblue, penwidth = 1]
    RRNA [label = 'rRNA\nTranscriptome'];
    HBRNA [label = 'hbRNA\nTranscriptome'];

    # Reporting Nodes
    node [shape = diamond, color = forestgreen, fontcolor = forestgreen, penwidth = 2]
    DEPLETION_QC [label = 'Depletion Checks']

    HEAD -> SUBFQ
    {SUBFQ RRNA} -> BWA1 -> RRNABAM -> FLAGSTAT1 -> RRNAFLAGSTATS ->  DEPLETION_QC
    {SUBFQ HBRNA} -> BWA2 -> HBRNABAM -> FLAGSTAT2 -> HBRNAFLAGSTATS ->  DEPLETION_QC

    fontsize=30;
    label = 'Depletion Checks';
    color = grey;

  }


  subgraph cluster_salmon {

      # Key Data Nodes
      node [shape = box, penwidth = 3]
      TRANS_COUNTS [label = 'Transcript-Level\nCounts']

      # Process Nodes
      node [shape = ellipse, penwidth = 1, color = red, fontcolor = red]
      SALMON [label = 'salmon']

      # Minor Data Nodes
      node [shape = box, fontcolor = grey30, color = grey30, penwidth = 1]
      SALMON_METRICS [label = 'salmon\nQC metrics']

      # Reporting Nodes
      node [shape = diamond, color = forestgreen, fontcolor = forestgreen, penwidth = 2]
      SALMON_QC [label = 'Salmon QC']

      # External Inputs
      node [shape = box, color = navyblue, fontcolor = navyblue, penwidth = 1]
      FULL_TRANS_REF [label = 'Full & Y-Excluded\nConsensus Transcriptome']

      FULL_TRANS_REF -> SALMON -> {TRANS_COUNTS SALMON_METRICS}
      SALMON_METRICS -> SALMON_QC

      fontsize=30;
      label = 'Salmon';
      color = grey;

  }

  subgraph cluster_star {

      # Key Data Nodes
      node [shape = box, penwidth = 3]
      STAR_BAM [label = 'Aligned.bam']
      GENE_COUNTS [label = 'Gene-Level\nCounts']
      SJ [label = 'Splice Junctions']

      # Process Nodes
      node [shape = ellipse, penwidth = 1, color = red, fontcolor = red]
      STAR [label = 'STAR\n2 Pass Mode']
      SAMTOOLS [label = 'samtools']
      FCOUNTS [label = 'featureCounts']

      # Minor Data Nodes
      node [shape = box, fontcolor = grey30, color = grey30, penwidth = 1]
      STAR_BAI [label = 'Aligned.bam.bai']
      BAM_STATS [label = 'Samtools Stats']
      STAR_LOGS [label = 'STAR Logs']

      # External Inputs
      node [shape = box, color = navyblue, fontcolor = navyblue, penwidth = 1]
      FULL_REF [label = 'Full & Y-Masked\nConsensus Genome']
      FULL_GTF [label = 'Gencode GTF']
      FULL_REF -> STAR -> {SJ STAR_LOGS STAR_BAM}
      {STAR_BAM STAR_BAI FULL_GTF} -> FCOUNTS -> GENE_COUNTS
      STAR_BAM -> SAMTOOLS -> {STAR_BAI BAM_STATS}


      fontsize=30;
      label = 'STAR';
      color = grey;

  }

  subgraph cluster_idchecks {


      # Process Nodes
      node [shape = ellipse, penwidth = 1, color = red, fontcolor = red]
      BCFTOOLS [label = 'bcftools']

      # External Inputs
      node [shape = box, color = navyblue, fontcolor = navyblue, penwidth = 1]
      CONS_SNPS [label = 'Consensus SNPs']

      # Reporting Nodes
      node [shape = diamond, color = forestgreen, fontcolor = forestgreen, penwidth = 2]
      CHECK_VARS [label = 'Variant\nChecking']

      {STAR_BAM STAR_BAI CONS_SNPS} -> BCFTOOLS -> CHECK_VARS

      fontsize=30;
      label = 'Sample\nChecks';
      color = grey;

  }

  subgraph cluster_alnqc {

      # Key Data Nodes
      node [shape = box, penwidth = 3]
      BW [label = 'Coverage\nBigWig']

      # Process Nodes
      node [shape = ellipse, penwidth = 1, color = red, fontcolor = red]
      RSEQC [label = 'RSeQC']
      BEDOPTS [label = 'bedopts']
      BAM2BW [label = 'bam2wig']
      RNA_METRICS [label = 'tin\ninner_distance\nread_distribution']

      # Reporting Nodes
      node [shape = diamond, color = forestgreen, fontcolor = forestgreen, penwidth = 2]
      ALN_QC [label = 'Alignment QC']

      # External Inputs
      node [shape = box, color = navyblue, fontcolor = navyblue, penwidth = 1]
      GENE_BED [label = 'Gene BED File']

      FULL_GTF -> BEDOPTS -> GENE_BED
      {STAR_BAM GENE_BED} -> RSEQC -> RNA_METRICS
      {STAR_LOGS RNA_METRICS BAM_STATS} -> ALN_QC
      {STAR_BAM RSEQC} -> BAM2BW -> BW


      fontsize=30;
      label = 'Alignment\nQC';
      color = grey;

  }

  # Key Data Nodes
  node [shape = box, penwidth = 3]
  FQ_RAW [label = 'Raw FQ'];

  # Edges
  edge [penwidth=2]
  FQ_RAW -> {HEAD FP}
  FP -> FQ_PROC -> {SALMON STAR}


}
")
```

::: {.fragment .fade-in-then-out data-fragment-index="1"}
::: {.rect_outline .absolute bottom='150px' right='260px' width='200px' height='450px'}
:::
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in-then-out data-fragment-index="2"}
::: {.rect_outline .absolute top='90px' left='240px' width='140px' height='250px'}
:::
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in-then-out data-fragment-index="3"}
::: {.rect_outline .absolute top='190px' left='360px' width='140px' height='200px'}
:::
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in-then-out data-fragment-index="4"}
::: {.rect_outline .absolute top='180px' left='500px' width='160px' height='375px'}
:::
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in-then-out data-fragment-index="5"}
::: {.rect_outline .absolute bottom='180px' right='455px' width='160px' height='300px'}
:::
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in-then-out data-fragment-index="6"}
::: {.rect_outline .absolute top='380px' left='420px' width='100px' height='220px'}
:::
:::

## Pipeline Output

::: {style="font-size: 85%"}

```
/path/to/prophecy/PROP000000/ # PROPHECY ID
└── 1234567 # Aliquot ID
    └── RNASEQ
        ├── bigwigs
        │   └── E200099999 # Sequencing Run ID
        │       └── 23-00000 # ULN
        │           ├── GRCh38
        │           └── PROPHECY
        ├── depletion
        │   └── E200099999
        │       └── 23-00000
        │           ├── 1234567_1_hbrna.flagstat
        │           └── 1234567_1_rrna.flagstat
        ├── fastp
        │   └── E200099999
        │       └── 23-00000
        │           ├── 1234567_1_fastp.html
        │           └── 1234567_1_fastp.json
        ├── fastq
        │   └── E200099999
        │       └── 23-00000
        │           ├── 23-00000_1_S24_L01_R1_001.fastq.gz
        │           ├── 23-00000_1_S24_L01_R1_001.md5
        │           └── 23-00000_1_S24_L01_R2_001.fastq.gz
        ├── featureCounts
        │   └── E200099999
        │       └── 23-00000
        │           ├── GRCh38
        │           └── PROPHECY
        ├── rseqc
        │   └── E200099999
        │       └── 23-00000
        │           ├── GRCh38
        │           │   ├── inner_distance
        │           │   │   ├── 1234567_1.inner_distance_freq.txt
        │           │   │   ├── 1234567_1.inner_distance_mean.txt
        │           │   │   └── 1234567_1.inner_distance.txt
        │           │   ├── read_distribution
        │           │   │   └── 1234567_1.read_distribution.tsv
        │           │   └── tin
        │           │       ├── 1234567_1.summary.txt
        │           │       └── 1234567_1.tin.tsv
        │           └── PROPHECY
        ├── salmon
        │   └── E200099999
        │       └── 23-00000
        │           ├── GRCh38
        │           │   └── 1234567_1
        │           │       ├── aux_info
        │           │       ├── cmd_info.json
        │           │       ├── lib_format_counts.json
        │           │       ├── libParams
        │           │       ├── logs
        │           │       └── quant.sf        
        │           └── PROPHECY
        └── star
            └── E200099999
                └── 23-00000
                    ├── GRCh38
                    │   ├── 1234567_1.cram
                    │   ├── 1234567_1.cram.crai
                    │   ├── 1234567_1.cram.md5
                    │   ├── 1234567_1.Log.final.out
                    │   ├── 1234567_1.SJ.out.tab
                    │   ├── 1234567_1.stats
                    │   └── 1234567_1_strandcheckr.tsv.gz                    
                    └── PROPHECY
```

:::

- For all `r 13*96` libraries (including 1 repeated run ...)

## Final Outcome

- 31 sample mixups $\implies$ all resolved successfully
    + Only one library was an unexpected participant
    + 17 had no genotypes $\implies$ assumed correct
- 1 library broke `STARconsensus`
    + `DUG` team identified bug in `STAR` & made pull request
- Re-integrating mixups was non-trivial
    + QC reports written for 96 samples failed
    + Manual re-collation of all QC outputs

## Final Summary

::: {style = "font-size:60%"}

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Plate                Pass   Caution     Fail   Incomplete   Total Yield (billions)   Mean Q30
--------------- --------- --------- -------- ------------ ------------------------ ----------
SAGCQA0503-2           40        47        9            0                     6.26      94.8%

SAGCQA0503-3           92         4        0            0                     6.36      95.2%

SAGCQA0503-4           70        15       11            0                     6.14      94.4%

SAGCQA0503-5           68        18       10            0                     6.24      94.2%

SAGCQA0503-6           62        28        6            0                     6.31      94.7%

SAGCQA0503-7           77        11        8            0                     5.94      94.2%

SAGCQA0503-8           85         6        5            0                     5.51      92.4%

SAGCQA0503-9           92         1        3            0                     5.81      93.1%

SAGCQA0503-10          68        22        6            0                     5.69      93.3%

SAGCQA0503-11          88         7        0            1                     4.89      91.4%

SAGCQA0503-12          49        36       11            0                     5.83      93.9%

SAGCQA0503-13          90         3        3            0                     5.21      92.8%

SAGCQA0503-14          38        52        6            0                     6.31      94.7%

**Total**         **919**   **250**   **78**        **1**                                    
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

      
:::

## QC

::: {style = "font-size:47%"}

| Name | Source | Interpretation |
|:---- |:---- |:------------------ |
| `status` | Manual Inspection | Originally defined in order to identify libraries which might be candidates for resequencing |
| `rin`    | SAGC              | RNA Integrity Number with high values (> 7) indicating a higher quality sample |
| `conc`   | SAGC              | RNA Concentration in the original extractions |
| `depletion_conc`   | SAGC    | RNA Concentration used for the rRNA/hbRNA deletion step. Two values were used with the lower concentration being an attempt to reduce saturation of the depletion reagents |
| `rrna_rate` | BODL pipeline  | Estimates the proportion of reads deriving from rRNA by aligning a subset of reads to an rRNA reference |
| `hbrna_rate` | BODL pipeline | Estimates the proportion of reads deriving from hbRNA by aligning a subset of reads to an rRNA reference |
| `duplication_rate` | `fastp`   | Taken directly from the fastp reports, given that UMIs were included in all libraries |
| `sequenced_fragments` | `fastp` | The initial number of frgaments contained in each pair of fastq files |
| `q30_rate` | `fastp` | The proportion of bases acheiving a PHRED quality score > 30 when being sequenced |
| `gc_content` | `fastp` | The average GC content of reads across the entire library |
| `gene_assigned_rate` | `salmon` | `num_frags_with_concordant_consistent_mappings` / `sequenced_fragments`|
| `decoy_aligned_rate` | `salmon` | `num_decoy_fragments` / `sequenced_fragments` |
| `median_insert_size` | `samtools stats` | Calculated by only taking alignments which are concordantly paired |
| `sd_log10_insert_size` | `samtools stats` | Calculated by only taking alignments which are concordantly paired, the transforming the insert size using log~10~ and finding the standard deviation |
| `median_tin` | `nf-core` | The median TIN score across all measured trancripts |
| `prop_no_feature` | `featureCounts` | The proportion of input reads which aligned where there was no annotated feature |
| `stranded_coverage` | `strandcheckR` | The proportion of windows showing >90% of reads with a stranded bias, weighted by coverage |
| `prop_assigned` | `featureCounts` / `salmon` | The proportion of input reads which were assigned to a gene/transcript |

:::

## QC: Duplication Rates

![](images/duplication-by-plate-1.png)

## QC: rRNA Content

![](images/compare-rrna-1.png)

## QC: Strandedness of Alignments

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column}

![](images/stranded-coverage-1.png)
:::
::: {.column}

::: {.fragment}

![](images/gdna-by-plate-1.png)
:::
:::
::::

## QC: Strandedness Vs Insert Size Variability

![](images/gdna-vs-ins-var-1.png)

## QC: % Of Sequenced Reads Assigned to Genes

![](images/fc-assigned-1.png)

## Similarity of QC Metrics

![](images/corrplot-1.png)

## Outlier Detection

1. Run PCA
2. Find Robust Z-scores for PC1-4
3. Label as "No", "Potential" or "Unambiguous" Outlier
    + |Z| > 1/200 (`r round(qnorm(0.995), 4)`) or |Z| > 1/2000 (`r round(qnorm(0.9995), 4)`)
4. Find which QC metrics have outlier threshold

## Outlier Detection

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column}

![Outliers at low values](images/low-metric-thresholds-1.png)
:::
::: {.column}
![Outliers at high values](images/high-metric-thresholds-1.png)

:::
::::

## Outlier Detection

![](images/upset-key-vars-1.png){.absolute left=0 top=100 width='900px'}

::: {.fragment}

![](https://smped.github.io/SimpleUpset/reference/figures/SimpleUpset.png){.absolute right=100 top=100 width='150px'}

:::

## Outlier Detection

![](images/final-pca-1.png)

## Normalisation

- Do we have batch effects?
    + `hRUV` [@Kim2021-kz], `RUV` [@Risso2014-qg], `RUVIII-NB` [@Salim2022-rj]
    + TMM [@Robinson2010-qp], `CQN` [@Hansen2012-jz]
- `hRUV` hard-wired to USyd metabolomics structure
    + Rewrote significant amount of (github-only) package
- `RUVIII-NB` also needed rewriting (github only)

::: {.fragment}
- Didn't need them anyway &#128517;
:::

## Batch Effects

![](images/pca-raw-1.png)

## GC & Length Artefacts

::: {style="font-size:90%"}

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width='60%'}

![](images/combined-rotations-1.png)
:::
::: {.column width='40%'}
::: {.incremental}
- Bin gene-level rotations from PCA by GC & Length
- Perform $t$-test in each bin
- If no bias $\implies$ random noise
- Looks like GC & length is impacting signal
:::
:::
::::
:::

## Conditional Quantile Normalisation

![PC4 captures age &#127881;](images/pca-cqn-1.png){fig-align="left"}

## Performance on Pools

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
![](images/tmm-pools-1.png)
:::

::: {.column}
![](images/cqn-pools-1.png)
:::
::::

Both improve further using `glmTreat()` at FC > 1.1

## Do Any QC Metrics Impact Signal

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width='60%'}

![](images/complete-metrics-plot-1.png)
:::
::: {.column width='40%'}
- Several QC metrics strongly correlate with PC1-3
- How will this impact DGE analysis?
- Can I check?
:::
::::


## Using QC Metrics As Predictors

```{r}
tbl <- structure(list(x = list(tag = structure(list(name = "Reactable", 
    attribs = list(data = structure("{\"coef\":[\"Prop No Feature\",\"Gene Assigned Rate\",\"Prop Assigned\",\"RIN\",\"SD log10 Insert Size\",\"Median Insert Size\",\"Decoy Aligned Rate\",\"hbRNA Rate\",\"GC Content\",\"Median Tin\",\"Conc\",\"Q30 Rate\",\"Depletion Conc\",\"Duplication Rate\",\"rRNA Rate\",\"Stranded Coverage\",\"Sequenced Fragments\"],\"nDE\":[11816,11684,11655,11537,11494,11276,11023,10672,8606,8243,6728,5152,5083,3599,1601,1060,171],\"top_gene\":[\"ENSG00000281195\",\"TALAM1\",\"TALAM1\",\"TBCEL\",\"CENATAC\",\"PABPN1\",\"ENSG00000280287\",\"HBA1\",\"ENSG00000290038\",\"CHMP4A\",\"TIMM8B\",\"RPL13AP5\",\"RPSA2\",\"ENSG00000290038\",\"MT-RNR1\",\"TALAM1\",\"RPL13AP5\"],\"gene_type\":[\"lncRNA\",\"lncRNA\",\"lncRNA\",\"protein_coding\",\"protein_coding\",\"protein_coding\",\"TEC\",\"protein_coding\",\"lncRNA\",\"protein_coding\",\"protein_coding\",\"processed_pseudogene\",\"protein_coding\",\"lncRNA\",\"Mt_rRNA\",\"lncRNA\",\"processed_pseudogene\"],\"Q1\":[0.205357142857143,0.204928571428571,0.2055,0.194428571428571,0.2055,0.191714285714286,0.195214285714286,0.169642857142857,0.159,0.116785714285714,0.101571428571429,0.0610714285714286,0.0775,0.0429285714285714,0.031,0.0271428571428571,0.00421428571428571],\"Q2\":[0.211857142857143,0.209857142857143,0.209428571428571,0.206785714285714,0.207857142857143,0.200642857142857,0.1985,0.192571428571429,0.155214285714286,0.145785714285714,0.119357142857143,0.0904285714285714,0.0872142857142857,0.054,0.0241428571428571,0.0181428571428571,0.00321428571428571],\"Q3\":[0.213142857142857,0.206642857142857,0.205428571428571,0.209357142857143,0.203142857142857,0.204857142857143,0.195714285714286,0.199428571428571,0.150857142857143,0.159214285714286,0.125214285714286,0.1095,0.0976428571428571,0.076,0.0285714285714286,0.0156428571428571,0.00192857142857143],\"Q4\":[0.213642857142857,0.213142857142857,0.212142857142857,0.2135,0.2045,0.208214285714286,0.197928571428571,0.200642857142857,0.149642857142857,0.167,0.134428571428571,0.107,0.100714285714286,0.0841428571428571,0.0306428571428571,0.0147857142857143,0.00285714285714286],\"mean_gc\":[0.485230896801646,0.485678531294568,0.48547096565743,0.485072943195065,0.485202804529892,0.485388346965225,0.484716074796123,0.48655230634273,0.48558842498627,0.487858954154054,0.486435199632921,0.488509217881652,0.480068635699605,0.473266997959184,0.492580498622014,0.503611422238164,0.474119597361045],\"mean_length\":[4719.40206652058,4699.18761329903,4702.56812878586,4703.49289132616,4733.74010577661,4711.29441955981,4722.3297896665,4804.99750141362,4742.3146889368,4485.99394000667,4391.75429862889,4368.27850755664,4490.78023178923,4552.71940573309,4042.61614488541,5316.11786663301,1522.59285688669]}", class = "json"), 
        columns = list(list(id = "coef", name = "QC Metric", 
            type = "character"), list(id = "nDE", name = "DE Genes", 
            type = "numeric", format = list(cell = structure(list(
                separators = TRUE), class = "colFormat"), aggregated = structure(list(
                separators = TRUE), class = "colFormat"))), list(
            id = "top_gene", name = "Highest Ranked Gene", type = "character", 
            cell = list(structure(list(name = "em", attribs = structure(list(), names = character(0)), 
                children = list("ENSG00000281195")), class = "shiny.tag"), 
                structure(list(name = "em", attribs = structure(list(), names = character(0)), 
                  children = list("TALAM1")), class = "shiny.tag"), 
                structure(list(name = "em", attribs = structure(list(), names = character(0)), 
                  children = list("TALAM1")), class = "shiny.tag"), 
                structure(list(name = "em", attribs = structure(list(), names = character(0)), 
                  children = list("TBCEL")), class = "shiny.tag"), 
                structure(list(name = "em", attribs = structure(list(), names = character(0)), 
                  children = list("CENATAC")), class = "shiny.tag"), 
                structure(list(name = "em", attribs = structure(list(), names = character(0)), 
                  children = list("PABPN1")), class = "shiny.tag"), 
                structure(list(name = "em", attribs = structure(list(), names = character(0)), 
                  children = list("ENSG00000280287")), class = "shiny.tag"), 
                structure(list(name = "em", attribs = structure(list(), names = character(0)), 
                  children = list("HBA1")), class = "shiny.tag"), 
                structure(list(name = "em", attribs = structure(list(), names = character(0)), 
                  children = list("ENSG00000290038")), class = "shiny.tag"), 
                structure(list(name = "em", attribs = structure(list(), names = character(0)), 
                  children = list("CHMP4A")), class = "shiny.tag"), 
                structure(list(name = "em", attribs = structure(list(), names = character(0)), 
                  children = list("TIMM8B")), class = "shiny.tag"), 
                structure(list(name = "em", attribs = structure(list(), names = character(0)), 
                  children = list("RPL13AP5")), class = "shiny.tag"), 
                structure(list(name = "em", attribs = structure(list(), names = character(0)), 
                  children = list("RPSA2")), class = "shiny.tag"), 
                structure(list(name = "em", attribs = structure(list(), names = character(0)), 
                  children = list("ENSG00000290038")), class = "shiny.tag"), 
                structure(list(name = "em", attribs = structure(list(), names = character(0)), 
                  children = list("MT-RNR1")), class = "shiny.tag"), 
                structure(list(name = "em", attribs = structure(list(), names = character(0)), 
                  children = list("TALAM1")), class = "shiny.tag"), 
                structure(list(name = "em", attribs = structure(list(), names = character(0)), 
                  children = list("RPL13AP5")), class = "shiny.tag")), 
            html = TRUE, minWidth = 100), list(id = "gene_type", 
            name = "Gene Type", type = "character"), list(id = "Q1", 
            name = "Q1", type = "numeric", format = list(cell = structure(list(
                digits = 1L, percent = TRUE), class = "colFormat"), 
                aggregated = structure(list(digits = 1L, percent = TRUE), class = "colFormat")), 
            maxWidth = 75), list(id = "Q2", name = "Q2", type = "numeric", 
            format = list(cell = structure(list(digits = 1L, 
                percent = TRUE), class = "colFormat"), aggregated = structure(list(
                digits = 1L, percent = TRUE), class = "colFormat")), 
            maxWidth = 75), list(id = "Q3", name = "Q3", type = "numeric", 
            format = list(cell = structure(list(digits = 1L, 
                percent = TRUE), class = "colFormat"), aggregated = structure(list(
                digits = 1L, percent = TRUE), class = "colFormat")), 
            maxWidth = 75), list(id = "Q4", name = "Q4", type = "numeric", 
            format = list(cell = structure(list(digits = 1L, 
                percent = TRUE), class = "colFormat"), aggregated = structure(list(
                digits = 1L, percent = TRUE), class = "colFormat")), 
            maxWidth = 75), list(id = "mean_gc", name = "Mean GC Content", 
            type = "numeric", format = list(cell = structure(list(
                digits = 1L, percent = TRUE), class = "colFormat"), 
                aggregated = structure(list(digits = 1L, percent = TRUE), class = "colFormat")), 
            maxWidth = 90), list(id = "mean_length", name = "Mean DE Gene Length", 
            type = "numeric", format = list(cell = structure(list(
                digits = 0L), class = "colFormat"), aggregated = structure(list(
                digits = 0L), class = "colFormat")), maxWidth = 90)), 
        pagination = FALSE, theme = structure(list(style = list(
            fontSize = 14)), class = "reactableTheme"), dataKey = "df90639d970ab153d63431dff8ae439c", 
        static = FALSE), children = list()), class = c("reactR_component", 
"shiny.tag", "list")), class = "reactR_markup"), width = NULL, 
    height = NULL, sizingPolicy = list(defaultWidth = "auto", 
        defaultHeight = "auto", padding = NULL, fill = NULL, 
        viewer = list(defaultWidth = NULL, defaultHeight = NULL, 
            padding = NULL, fill = TRUE, suppress = FALSE, paneHeight = NULL), 
        browser = list(defaultWidth = NULL, defaultHeight = NULL, 
            padding = NULL, fill = FALSE, external = FALSE), 
        knitr = list(defaultWidth = NULL, defaultHeight = NULL, 
            figure = FALSE)), dependencies = list(), elementId = NULL, 
    preRenderHook = NULL, jsHooks = list()), class = c("reactable", 
"htmlwidget"), package = "reactable")
browsable(tagList(tbl))
```


## Managing Noise

- Strategy is to fit a null (intercept-only) model
    + Add QC metrics to find which improve fit <br>$\implies$ becomes baseline model for all DGE analysis
    + Identify best combination of metrics
- gDNA-related metrics (QC PC1)
    + `stranded_coverage`, `prop_no_feature`, `sd_log10_insert_size`, `decoy_aligned_rate`, `gene_assigned_rate`
- Library composition metrics (QC PC2)
    + `hbrna_rate`, `rrna_rate`, `gc_content`, `duplication_rate`
- Also tried RIN
  
## Managing Noise: gDNA Metrics

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
![Lower Dispersions $\implies$ better fit](images/gdna-resid-quartile-1.png)
:::
::: {.column}
::: {.fragment}
![Differences from intercept-only](images/gdna-dev-ratio-quartile-1.png)
:::
:::
::::
    
## Managing Noise: Library Composition

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
![Lower Dispersions $\implies$ better fit](images/comp-resid-quartile-1.png)
:::
::: {.column}
::: {.fragment}
![Differences from intercept-only](images/comp-dev-ratio-quartile-1.png)
:::
:::
::::    

## Managing Noise: Combined Metrics

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
![Lower Dispersions $\implies$ better fit](images/cand-dev-ratio-quartile-1.png)
:::
::: {.column}
::: {incremental}
- No combination outperformed `prop_no_feature` as a natural spline
    + Each predictor costs degrees of freedom
- Have also tested age as a predictor
    + Natural spline is better than linear
- PC1 from genotype data improves about 30% of genes

:::
:::
::::    

## Take Home

::: {style="font-size:95%"}

- Unlike any dataset I've ever worked with
- The gDNA work reinforces Hien To's work on `strandcheckR`
    + Would love to see it added to `nf-core`
- Setting all data handling early is great
    + Unexpected challenges incorporating mixups
    + Typos in sample sheets can be infuriating (e.g. Pool vs pool)
- Didn't see any clinical variables for >2yrs
    - T2D: 000's of DE Genes
    - CKD: 000's of DE Genes
    - CHD is a really difficult phenotype

:::

## Clickbait Time

Is large cohort transcriptomics just scRNA without the zero counts?

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width='40%'}

![Stevie speaks the truth](https://media.giphy.com/media/v1.Y2lkPWVjZjA1ZTQ3N3Q5cW0wMXloa2J2dzFwNTZqMm00NmE2cXV3cm5uZWVnOXlxM3hneCZlcD12MV9naWZzX3JlbGF0ZWQmY3Q9Zw/26hkhKd2Cp5WMWU1O/giphy.gif){fig-align="left"}
:::
::: {.column width='60%'}
The questions are completely different:

::: {.incremental}

- We want clusters of genes defining disease pathology with age + complication, NOT clusters of cells
- QC represents fundamentally different technical processes

:::
:::
::::

## Acknowledgements

::: {style="font-size:80%"}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width='25%'}
[**Black Ochre Data Labs**]{.underline}

Alex Brown

**Jimmy Breen**

**Alastair Ludington**

Sam Buckberry

Liza Kretzschmar

Yassine Souilmi

Katharine Brown

Rose Senesi

Sam Godwin

Rebecca Simpson

:::

::: {.column width='25%'}
<br>
Adam Heterick

Kaashifah Bruce

Bastien Llamas

Amanda Richards-Satour

Justine Clark

Holly Massacci

Sarah Munns

Alli Foster

Sehaj Dhariwal

:::

::: {.column width='25%'}
[**SAGC**]{.underline}

Sen Wang

Renee Smith

**John Salamon**

Paul Wang

[**Baker Heart & Diabetes Institute**]{.underline}

Sam El Osta

Ishant Kurma

Moshe Olshansky

Scott Maxwell

:::
::: {.column width='25%'}

[**SAHMRI / Wardliparingga**]{.underline}

**Natasha Howard**

[**Victor Chang Cardiac Research Institute**]{.underline}

Jason Kovacic

[**University of Sydney**]{.underline}

Jean Yang

[**Centre For Population Genomics**]{.underline}

Dan MacArthur


:::
::::

:::

## References
